asset_family: town forge equipment direction target and source families
audience: broad PC fantasy RPG players; serious readable browser RPG adventure, not satire, not idle, not debug UI

required_asset_groups.role: single composed target for the town forge upgrade state: forge/workbench in the scene, hero and blacksmith/warden focus, Mine Lantern object/result slot, ember shard cost, route strip showing Depth 2 opened, and persistent browser-RPG frame
required_asset_groups.content_policy: direction target only; do not treat as one fused runtime UI image
required_asset_groups.coordinate_policy: Y is up always. Composition notes, crop placement notes, and future runtime layout must remain Y-up; larger logical y is higher on the game screen. Any Y-down value is allowed only inside named renderer/input/screenshot/DevAPI boundary conversion code.

required_asset_groups.role: painted Old Gate forge/town workbench layer, lantern crafting focus, forge glow, and route/depth marker overlays
required_asset_groups.content_policy: no UI text, no baked labels, no copied reference landmarks
required_asset_groups.layer_policy: background, forge/workbench, lantern object, ember shard glow, and route-depth highlight should be separable or reproducible

required_asset_groups.role: Mine Lantern, ember shard, forge hammer/anvil, unlock/depth, cost paid, equipped result icons
required_asset_groups.content_policy: isolated icons with gutters; no numbers or labels baked into icons

required_asset_groups.role: isolated hero, blacksmith or Gate Warden forge pose, lantern pickup/equip marker, and small forge glow FX
required_asset_groups.anchor_policy: pivot or anchor must be recorded before runtime use

required_asset_groups.role: ornate but readable browser-RPG frame pieces for right equipment/result rail, route strip, bottom log, reward/equipment slots, and primary button bases
required_asset_groups.content_policy: runtime text/icons only inside safe areas
required_asset_groups.stretch_zone_policy: centers and long edges must be repeatable; unique ornaments stay in corners or separate overlays
required_asset_groups.decor_overlay_policy: quest gems, banners, medallions, locks, and center ornaments must be separate overlays or fixed corner pieces, never stretched through the center

final_asset_policy.notes: Code may cut, validate, pack, and compose. Code-generated/programmer art is not acceptable as final generated UI unless an explicit exception is recorded.

source_family_roles.blank UI kit sheet: stretchable bases only: panels, button states, bars, frames, dividers
source_family_roles.ui decor overlay sheet: non-stretch ornaments, gems, badges, caps, medallions with anchors
source_family_roles.isolated icon sheet: semantic icons with gutters and transparent/chroma source
source_family_roles.composed town forge fake shot: full-screen target composition for direction review only; not a fused runtime asset
source_family_roles.town forge background layer sheet: painted forge/workbench scene, lantern crafting focus, route-depth marker overlays
source_family_roles.forge lantern resource icon sheet: Mine Lantern, ember shard, forge, unlock/depth, equipped result icons with gutters
source_family_roles.town forge character sprite sheet: isolated hero, blacksmith or Gate Warden forge pose, lantern pickup/equip marker, and forge glow FX
source_family_roles.fantasy forge ui frame sheet: slice9-safe equipment/result rail, route strip, bottom log belt, reward slots, button states, and decor overlays

prompt_constraints: no fused icons inside button backgrounds
transparent or flat chroma-key background for isolated assets
no unique decoration inside slice9 stretch zones
no copied War of Dragons, Dragon Eternity, AQW, DragonFable, AdventureQuest, Shakes and Fidget, or Wartune UI ornament shapes
Y-up must be preserved in layout notes and runtime translation; larger logical y means higher on the game screen
forge/equipment meaning must be visible in the scene, not only in the right panel

expected_outputs.runtime_scope_note: Direction fake shot is accepted for review only. Runtime crop, source-family generation, slice9 metadata, atlas packing, native integration, and product gate proof are still required before final-art or gameplay expansion claims.

runtime_composition.layout_axis: Y-up in game/UI logic; larger logical y means higher on the game screen. Renderer, input, screenshot, and DevAPI may convert at named boundaries only; never store Y-down coordinates as layout truth.
runtime_composition.main_scene: painted Old Gate forge/workbench scene plus separate hero, blacksmith/warden, lantern object, ember shard glow, and route-depth markers
runtime_composition.quest_or_equipment_rail: slice9 rail plus runtime text, portrait/forge marker, lantern result slot, shard cost icons, and one primary button
runtime_composition.route_strip: destination plaques and depth marker composed over a route layer; Depth 2 unlocked after forge
runtime_composition.bottom_log: slice9 log/action belt plus runtime confirmation; log is confirmation, not the whole UX

qa_rejects: one fused screenshot used as runtime UI
text or labels baked into reusable pieces
Y-up assumptions inverted in layout notes
panel-only forge UX with no scene object anchor

commands.new_generation_record: node tools/assets/job/new_generation_record.mjs --id <source-id> --project-dir gamedesign/projects/ember-road --source-family "<source family>" --source-family-role "<source family role>" --accepted-source <path> --provider <provider> --model <model-or-workflow> --workflow-path <workflow.json> --prompt-packet gamedesign/projects/ember-road/art/prompts/<source-id>-prompt.json --seed <seed> --prompt "<prompt>" --negative-prompt "<negative prompt>"
commands.slice_assets: fill after source sheets are accepted
commands.build_pack: fill after pack builder is wired
